narrative.canopy: The monsoon doesn't simply fall here. It moves through in layers — canopy first, then the vine, then the soil — in a sequence that repeats for months. The humidity that results is not incidental to what pepper becomes. It is, partly, what pepper is.
narrative.land: Dark, loose, built from years of leaf litter no one cleared. It holds water the way a forest holds water: slowly, and for a long time. Pepper doesn't establish well in open ground. It prefers what's been left alone.
narrative.hand: The vines climb living trees — the older way, before plantations introduced a tidier geometry. Harvest comes once a year, in January. The picker carries back only what the forest gave willingly that morning: what was ripe, what was reachable, what was left after the forest took its share. There is no process document for this. The knowledge of which berry, which vine, which morning — this was accumulated over time, in this specific forest, and belongs to the person who has it.
